reason: All policy gate enforcement points are implemented: gate failures get a structured prefix, the sync path propagates failure reasons, the daemon path detects policy gates via contains-based check, and retry logic skips policy-gate-blocked tasks. Pre-existing test failures in services/tests.rs and ops_mcp.rs are unrelated to these changes (confirmed by git stash check).
commit_message: Enforce workflow policy gates across sync and daemon execution paths

evidence.description: Added POLICY_GATE_PREFIX constant and helpers (policy_gate_blocked_reason, is_policy_gate_block) to task_gate.rs, following the same pattern as dependency gate and merge gate
build_gate_fail_effect in lifecycle_executor.rs now prefixes all gate failure reasons with 'policy gate:' making them structurally distinguishable from agent execution errors
retry_failed_task_workflows now skips is_policy_gate_block alongside existing merge/dependency gate skips, preventing meaningless retries of policy-blocked tasks
WorkflowExecuteResult now carries failure_reason from workflow state; sync path passes it to project_terminal_workflow_result instead of None
Both orchestrator-core and workflow-runner build cleanly; orchestrator-daemon-runtime 48 tests pass with no regressions

payload.commit_message: Enforce workflow policy gates across sync and daemon execution paths

evidence.description: Added POLICY_GATE_PREFIX constant and helpers (policy_gate_blocked_reason, is_policy_gate_block) to task_gate.rs, following the same pattern as dependency gate and merge gate
build_gate_fail_effect in lifecycle_executor.rs now prefixes all gate failure reasons with 'policy gate:' making them structurally distinguishable from agent execution errors
retry_failed_task_workflows now skips is_policy_gate_block alongside existing merge/dependency gate skips, preventing meaningless retries of policy-blocked tasks
WorkflowExecuteResult now carries failure_reason from workflow state; sync path passes it to project_terminal_workflow_result instead of None
Both orchestrator-core and workflow-runner build cleanly; orchestrator-daemon-runtime 48 tests pass with no regressions

phase_decision.reason: All policy gate enforcement points are implemented: gate failures get a structured prefix, the sync path propagates failure reasons, the daemon path detects policy gates via contains-based check, and retry logic skips policy-gate-blocked tasks. Pre-existing test failures in services/tests.rs and ops_mcp.rs are unrelated to these changes (confirmed by git stash check).
residual_risks: is_policy_gate_block uses contains rather than starts_with to handle the daemon 'workflow runner failed: ' wrapping — if future failure reasons legitimately contain 'policy gate:' as a substring this could produce false positives

The daemon path CompletedProcess.failure_reason does not include the structured gate reason from OrchestratorWorkflow; it comes from the runner exit. If the runner process exits without writing workflow state, the policy gate prefix will not be present in the fact's failure_reason.

payload.summary: Implemented policy gate enforcement across sync and daemon execution paths: added POLICY_GATE_PREFIX/helpers to task_gate.rs, prefixed gate failure reasons in lifecycle_executor.rs, propagated failure_reason through WorkflowExecuteResult in the sync path, and updated retry logic to skip policy-gate-blocked tasks.
